channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This slick new service puts ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Wikipedia on the map
item.description: I don’t know about you, but I tend to think about my favorite tech tools as being split into two separate saucepans: the “classic” apps we’ve known and relied on for ages and then the newer “AI” apps that have shown up over the past several months to serve some super-specific purpose.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s hidden white noise feature may be just the productivity boost you need
item.description: As I write this, the most pleasing sound is washing over me—gentle waves ebbing and flowing onto the shore. Sadly, I’m not actually on some magnificent tropical beach. Instead, the sounds of the sea are being generated by my Mac.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SEC, DOJ investigate CrowdStrike deal with reseller Carahsoft
item.description: US feds are reportedly investigating a $32 million deal inked by CrowdStrike with a government reseller to provide cybersecurity tools for the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) — products the agency never used and said it didn’t even purchase.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: No, Apple and Meta aren’t making humanoid robots
item.description: Tech press-landi and the tech blogosphere were abuzz last week with news that both Apple, the company that makes the iPhone, and Meta, the company that makes everybody on Facebook confront a daily flood of horrible AI-generated slop, are making full-size, humanoid robots.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI humanoid robots inch their way toward the workforce
item.description: AI-based humanoid robots could soon start becoming a workforce reality, thanks to parallel advances in generative AI and electromechanical components and mechatronics, enabling robots to have reasoning skills as well as physical abilities that exceed their predecessors.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Better student housing with a unified data platform
item.description: At the end of 2023, Chicago-based Article Student Living was acquired by a global real estate investment company, which allowed the business to expand, and enabled it to make key investments in the high-demand student housing market.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: Swedish authorities say they are investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GenAI is my copilot? Developers play whack-a-hallucination
item.description: Developers are quicker than most to embrace generative AI tools as part of their workflow. That can mean enhanced productivity and easing cognitive burdens, but it also means learning new technologies while they’re still being built.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Eurofirms acelera los procesos de selección mediante IA generativa
item.description: Eurofirms People First y Telefónica Tech han suscrito un acuerdo para aplicar inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa a los procesos de selección de la plantilla y evolucionar la gestión de los recursos humanos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bosch impulsa ‘6G Smart’ para mejorar la inspección visual de sus líneas de producción
item.description: El sector industrial continúa desplegando casos de uso donde la tecnología sirve de palanca para mejorar procesos internos y robustecer la experiencia del empleado. En este sentido, Bosch, junto al centro de investigación i2CAT, ha puesto en marcha el proyecto 6G Smart.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: INE Security’s Cybersecurity and IT Training Enhances Career Stability in Tech
item.description: 2025 marks a time of unprecedented volatility in the technology job market. On one hand, dependence on technology is soaring. The growth of AI and machine learning is propelling a surge in new technologies, tactics, and ideas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 5 takeaways from Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) on Thursday evening with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw and discuss a range of topics.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, The Hill

item.title: Steve Jobs a perfect choice for California’s $1 innovation coin
item.description: An iconic image of the late co-founder of Apple will be featured on the coin, which is expected to be released next year.
item.dc:creator: Sal Pizarro, The Mercury News

item.title: AT&T debuts SplitPay, making it easier to split the bill on shared plans
item.description: The upside of friends, roommates, or family members sharing a multiline phone plan is everyone has a lower monthly bill. The downside, however, is one person is ultimately responsible for both paying that bill—and gathering what is owed to him or her from their friends each month.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition
item.description: Learn how OSINT is shaping the future of intelligence and security. The post OSINT Unveiled: The Technology Behind the Intelligence – ODNI Edition first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Playing by the rules of AI
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Juan Orlandini, Fast Company

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: iPhones may soon be able to use satellite connections indoors
item.description: Sending messages via satellite on iPhones should soon be as easy to as using your mobile network signal, as Apple and satellite partner Globalstar put a new satellite constellation into space.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft’s quantum ambition: One million qubits in the palm of your hand
item.description: Microsoft announced a breakthrough in quantum computing today, unveiling a new kind of quantum processing unit, using a new type of material, to create what it says is a “radically different type of qubit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smart home device maker exposes 2.7 billion records in huge data breach
item.description: Major IoT data breach: Mars Hydro database with 2.7 billion records exposed. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the breach is a reminder of the risks that come with using IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
